title: incident-bot: lightweight diagnostic toolbelt + ephemeral debug sessions for responders
preview: Motivation: on-call responders repeatedly run the same set of manual diagnostics when an alert fires (span tailing, recent-error log windows, lightweight heap/alloc snapshots, and quick-curl checks). This PR introduces a compact 'diagnostic toolbelt' exposed by incident-bot and an ephemeral debug session mode that l...
text: Motivation: on-call responders repeatedly run the same set of manual diagnostics when an alert fires (span tailing, recent-error log windows, lightweight heap/alloc snapshots, and quick-curl checks). This PR introduces a compact 'diagnostic toolbelt' exposed by incident-bot and an ephemeral debug session mode that lets responders run short-lived, auditable diagnostics without escalation to infra. Goals: reduce MTTD by providing one-click diagnostics, keep operations safe via TTLs and RBAC checks, and ensure cost caps through soft quotas and telemetry. What this change does: 1) Adds a new /v1/incidents/{id}/toolbelt API surface that aggregates a configurable set of diagnostics (logs tail, span tail, recent-errors, quick-rerun of failing healthcheck endpoints, and a heap-lite snapshot request). 2) Implements ephemeral debug sessions: a generated session token with a 15-minute TTL that g...

title: heuristic DAG runbook compiler and local repro hints
preview: Motivation: Oncall responders frequently need a concise, executable set of steps that adapt to observed telemetry. This PR introduces a heuristic DAG compiler that converts high-level runbook templates and telemetry predicates into compact conditional playbooks plus short local repro hints (minimal traces, query sni...
text: Motivation: Oncall responders frequently need a concise, executable set of steps that adapt to observed telemetry. This PR introduces a heuristic DAG compiler that converts high-level runbook templates and telemetry predicates into compact conditional playbooks plus short local repro hints (minimal traces, query snippets, and service call stubs). Design: 1) A lightweight DAG IR captures nodes (check, query, mitigation, escalate) and edges with guard predicates; 2) Compiler heuristics prune nodes unlikely to apply given recent telemetry (configurable thresholds) and collapse linear chains into single composite actions to reduce cognitive load; 3) Local repro hint extractor synthesizes a minimal set of queries/trace spans and a docker-compose sandbox recipe (when available) to reproduce the symptom locally; 4) Safe-mode runner executes a dry-run passing through the DAG with simulated te...

title: runbook-telemetry-heuristics-and-blindspot-prober
preview: Problem: our runbook suggestion flow surface-level checks (restarts, scaling, config flags) but frequently misses gaps in telemetry — 'blindspots' where a missing or mis-tagged metric/tracing span causes responders to chase symptoms. This PR adds a lightweight heuristics engine that analyzes incoming incident contex...
text:
Problem: our runbook suggestion flow surface-level checks (restarts, scaling, config flags) but frequently misses gaps in telemetry — 'blindspots' where a missing or mis-tagged metric/tracing span causes responders to chase symptoms. This PR adds a lightweight heuristics engine that analyzes incoming incident context + historical telemetry expectations and surfaces likely blindspots, and a safe 'blindspot prober' that can run read-only checks against telemetry pipelines to validate assumptions.

What this does (high level):
- Adds a telemetry heuristics module to incident-bot that computes a blindspot_score for an incident based on absent expected signals (metrics/traces/log markers) and signal-quality indicators (sample rates, tag variance, TTL gaps).
- Introduces a blindspot prober that runs ephemeral, read-only queries (metrics histogram checks, span-presence checks, and tag-resolu...

title: inline-lint-orb-and-dependency-choreography: compact presubmit adapter + churn detector
preview: Motivation: our presubmit and lint surface has grown many small, slightly divergent composite actions and ad-hoc dependency rollup jobs. This PR introduces an "inline lint orb" (composite action pattern kept in-repo for now) and a small "dependency choreography" toolset that groups dependency updates into ecosystem-...
text:
Motivation: our presubmit and lint surface has grown many small, slightly divergent composite actions and ad-hoc dependency rollup jobs. This PR introduces an "inline lint orb" (composite action pattern kept in-repo for now) and a small "dependency choreography" toolset that groups dependency updates into ecosystem-aware batches, plus a churn detector to reduce noisy dep churn PRs. Goals: 1) shrink presubmit job graph and reduce redundant installs, 2) create a single reusable lint adapter that supports language-specific shims, 3) reduce dependency churn and make rollups deterministic, 4) provide a light-weight cache priming step to improve presubmit latency.

What changed (high level):
- Added a composite-ish action under .github/actions/inline-lint-orb that centralizes linters (eslint, go vet, rustfmt) with a small shim to select language adapters at runtime. This replaces three sepa...

title: Surge orchestration conductor and tenant telemetry bridge
preview: Motivation: transient multi-tenant microbursts frequently cause noisy autoscaler churn and downstream p95 latency spikes. This PR introduces a lightweight Surge Orchestration Conductor that coordinates short-lived surge commitments across pools, plus a tenant telemetry bridge that exports compact utilization fingerp...
text: Motivation: transient multi-tenant microbursts frequently cause noisy autoscaler churn and downstream p95 latency spikes. This PR introduces a lightweight Surge Orchestration Conductor that coordinates short-lived surge commitments across pools, plus a tenant telemetry bridge that exports compact utilization fingerprints for correlated analysis. The goal is to reduce reactive overprovisioning, improve tenant isolation during bursts, and provide richer signals for the autoscaler without increasing metric cardinality. Changes include: 1) a conductor control loop that accepts tentative surge offers, serializes commit windows, and emits lease intents to pool allocators; 2) a tenant isolation gate that computes a per-tenant isolation score and enforces soft caps when isolation threshold breached; 3) a utilization-fingerprint exporter that samples the per-tenant seqlen/throughput/burstiness...

title: tooling/execution: concurrency safeguards and tool-result-injection ergonomics
preview: Motivation: Customers integrating complex tool chains reported flaky outputs when multiple tools ran in parallel and tool results were injected back into the model stream. This PR standardizes execution semantics for multi-tool calls, adds runtime safeguards for parallelism, and harmonizes behavior with our OpenAI-c...
text: Motivation: Customers integrating complex tool chains reported flaky outputs when multiple tools ran in parallel and tool results were injected back into the model stream. This PR standardizes execution semantics for multi-tool calls, adds runtime safeguards for parallelism, and harmonizes behavior with our OpenAI-compat surface. Summary of changes: 1) Executor: introduce a per-request "tool-parallelism" cap (configurable at API/route level) that bounds concurrent tool invocations and provides backpressure hooks to the scheduler; 2) Injection protocol: formalize a deterministic injection ordering when multiple tool results arrive within the same model turn and attach an idempotency token to each injected result so retries do not duplicate content; 3) Retry semantics: new retry policy enum (none, best-effort, strict-idempotent) exposed via the API and respected by the runtime and OpenA...

title: stabilize-cuda-graph-capture-with-deterministic-kernel-mapping-and-shape-buckets
preview: Motivation: Recent rolling workloads exposed flakiness and performance regressions in CUDA graph capture/replay under our continuous-batching scheduler. Two root causes were observed: (1) non-deterministic kernel selection when capture order varied across worker threads, causing spurious re-captures and kernel misma...
text:
Motivation: Recent rolling workloads exposed flakiness and performance regressions in CUDA graph capture/replay under our continuous-batching scheduler. Two root causes were observed: (1) non-deterministic kernel selection when capture order varied across worker threads, causing spurious re-captures and kernel mismatches; (2) overly fine-grained shape boundaries that forced frequent warmups for near-identical shapes.

Summary: This PR introduces a deterministic kernel mapping table persisted per-worker for the life of the process (with safe invalidation), and a shape-bucketing fallback that groups close sequence/kv-cache shapes into conservative buckets used during capture selection. The scheduler is updated to prefer forming capture groups with identical buckets where possible, and we add a lightweight capture-time verification check that triggers a localized replay fallback instead ...

title: Introduce quiet-window sampling and Mosaic metric adapters for token burst noise suppression
preview: Motivation: production token bursts from background jobs and chatfanout generate high-cardinality short-lived noise that skews percentile-based alerts and dilutes useful tail traces. This PR introduces two complementary pieces: 1) a configurable quiet-window sampler that temporarily de-emphasizes bursts matching sho...
text: Motivation: production token bursts from background jobs and chatfanout generate high-cardinality short-lived noise that skews percentile-based alerts and dilutes useful tail traces. This PR introduces two complementary pieces: 1) a configurable quiet-window sampler that temporarily de-emphasizes bursts matching short high-throughput token patterns so SLO signals reflect real user-facing latency/cost instead of backend batch noise; 2) Mosaic metric adapters, a lightweight adapter layer that coalesces related token-phase counters, kernel latency samples, and queueing gauges into a small set of composite metrics and tags for dashboards and alert rules. Approach: quiet-window sampling detects burst windows per-route and per-tenant using a sliding token-rate estimator and applies an adjustable attenuation factor to sampling probability and cost attribution for spans that are classified as...

title: tenant-graceful-shepherd-and-circuit-unplug-hotfix
preview: Motivation: production incidents in the past 48 hours revealed two correlated failure modes during tenant unplug (customer-initiated detach and scheduled maintenance): 1) abrupt KV cache eviction left in-flight requests failing with 503s; 2) regional circuit state machines could flip to OPEN due to transient health ...
text: Motivation: production incidents in the past 48 hours revealed two correlated failure modes during tenant unplug (customer-initiated detach and scheduled maintenance): 1) abrupt KV cache eviction left in-flight requests failing with 503s; 2) regional circuit state machines could flip to OPEN due to transient health probe flaps and then remain sticky, causing cascading regional failover and load oscillation. This PR provides a minimal, low-risk hotfix that introduces a ’graceful shepherd’ flow for tenant unplug and hardens the circuit state transition logic to tolerate transient probe failures. Summary of changes: - Introduces a tenant-unplug coordinator that drains inflight shared KV cache sessions and transitions tenant routing into a ’soft-standby’ window (configurable ttl) before full detach. This avoids dropping requests and allows in-progress batches to complete. - Adds a transie...

title: Introduce frugal regimen defaults catalog and dry-run econ simulator
preview: Context and motivation: We need a first-class, opinionated set of 'frugal regimen' defaults that are safe for managed tenants to adopt out-of-the-box and a simulator that can estimate unit-econ impact before rollout. This PR introduces: 1) a defaults catalog (catalog/v1) that encodes batching, prefill/cache hysteres...
text: Context and motivation: We need a first-class, opinionated set of 'frugal regimen' defaults that are safe for managed tenants to adopt out-of-the-box and a simulator that can estimate unit-econ impact before rollout. This PR introduces: 1) a defaults catalog (catalog/v1) that encodes batching, prefill/cache hysteresis, and quantization profile suggestions as named presets; 2) a CLI dry-run simulator (cmd/econ-dryrun) that replays representative tenant request traces against a chosen preset to produce P50/P95 latency, token-cost, and estimated per-request unit econ; 3) telemetry hooks and tracing tags so the console can visualize simulated vs actual outcomes; 4) migration and schema validation tooling to safely add presets in future. Design notes: - Presets are deliberately conservative: safe fall-through to baseline model and non-destructive cache settings. - Simulator runs in a sandb...

title: Orchestra pausepoint harness with transparent rewind and cohort quarantine
preview:
Motivation:
We need a deterministic way to hold, inspect, and safely rewind model rollout traffic for live canaries and A/B experiments without losing session-affinity or producing silent regressions. Current pause mechanics are ad-hoc (manual scaling, pod cordon) and opaque in telemetry which makes post-hoc root ca...
text:
Motivation:
We need a deterministic way to hold, inspect, and safely rewind model rollout traffic for live canaries and A/B experiments without losing session-affinity or producing silent regressions. Current pause mechanics are ad-hoc (manual scaling, pod cordon) and opaque in telemetry which makes post-hoc root cause and safe rewind labor-intensive.

What this PR does (summary):
- Introduces a Pausepoint state machine inside the orchestrator that exposes explicit holdpoints for staged rollouts. Holdpoints can be triggered by policy (telemetry gates) or manually from the Console.
- Implements a Cohort Quarantine controller that isolates affected user slices when a holdpoint is entered; quarantined cohorts are routed to a shadow or golden variant, and traffic is stamped for replay.
- Adds Transparent Rewind: deterministic session-preserving replay of quarantined traffic against previo...

title: hook-priority-scheduler-and-template-dependency-resolution
preview: Introduce a hook priority scheduler and deterministic template dependency resolution for the private installer Helm charts and hook jobs. Motivation: tenants with large numbers of tenant-scoped init hooks were occasionally triggering concurrent heavy migrations and image pulls that caused network/registry rate limit...
text: Introduce a hook priority scheduler and deterministic template dependency resolution for the private installer Helm charts and hook jobs. Motivation: tenants with large numbers of tenant-scoped init hooks were occasionally triggering concurrent heavy migrations and image pulls that caused network/registry rate limits and prolonged upgrade windows. This PR adds (1) a scheduler that assigns hook execution windows and priority buckets to init hooks via values.yaml configuration, (2) deterministic template dependency graph resolution so hooks declared with 'requires' ordering are applied consistently across helm renders and upgrades, and (3) an optional init-rate-limiter sidecar controller integration that coordinates cross-tenant concurrency using a ConfigMap-backed lease. The change includes: - new values schema fields (hookGroups, priorityBuckets, templateDeps, rateLimiter) with valida...

title: Introduce contextual ballot evaluator and safety-snapshot regression gate
preview: Motivation: CI-driven functional regressions occasionally pass simple metric thresholds but still cause low-probability failure modes (safety, hallucination) to regress. This PR adds two complementary CI primitives: a Contextual Ballot Evaluator (CBE) that runs configurable re-samples of model responses and decides ...
text:
Motivation: CI-driven functional regressions occasionally pass simple metric thresholds but still cause low-probability failure modes (safety, hallucination) to regress. This PR adds two complementary CI primitives: a Contextual Ballot Evaluator (CBE) that runs configurable re-samples of model responses and decides pass/fail by consensus ballots weighted by semantic similarity and prompt cohort importance; and a Safety Snapshot Gate that captures classifier score distributions at merge time and raises soft-fail alerts when tail mass shifts beyond tolerance.

What this change does: Adds a ballot aggregation engine with pluggable weighting (semantic similarity, precedent weight, and token-length normalization), a CI hook that executes N reruns in parallel with staggered seeds and computes vote distributions, and a safety snapshot artifact that is stored alongside CI artifacts for histor...

title: staggered-concurrency-simulator-and-kernel-affinity-replayer
preview: Motivation: Our existing workload runners produce steady-state and bursty traffic, but they don't model situations where multiple client cohorts arrive in staggered waves with different concurrency profiles and pinned kernel/NUMA affinities. This PR introduces a new simulator and reporting pipeline that: 1) generate...
text:
Motivation: Our existing workload runners produce steady-state and bursty traffic, but they don't model situations where multiple client cohorts arrive in staggered waves with different concurrency profiles and pinned kernel/NUMA affinities. This PR introduces a new simulator and reporting pipeline that: 1) generates multi-cohort, staggered-concurrency workloads (wave-based arrival) with configurable inter-wave gaps and tail-heavy sub-cohorts; 2) models kernel affinity and scheduler-level contention across heterogeneous serving nodes (GPU+CPU mixes) so we can attribute latency spikes to kernel-level queuing vs runtime scheduling; 3) outputs compact heatmaps and per-kernel flamepoint snapshots compatible with our telemetry exporter so downstream dashboards can surface contention hotspots and affinity misconfigurations.

What this PR changes (high level): - benchmark-lab/sim/staggered_s...

title: Introduce staggered probing schedule and failure-decay gate
preview: Summary: Adds a time-staggered probing scheduler and a failure-decay gate to the eval CI pipeline. The staggered prober spreads probe execution across configurable windows to avoid thundering on sensitive model endpoints and to produce smoother rolling signals. The failure-decay gate introduces an exponentially deca...
text:
Summary: Adds a time-staggered probing scheduler and a failure-decay gate to the eval CI pipeline. The staggered prober spreads probe execution across configurable windows to avoid thundering on sensitive model endpoints and to produce smoother rolling signals. The failure-decay gate introduces an exponentially decaying penalty for isolated failures so that transient noise does not immediately block rollouts.
Motivation: We observed several incidents where atomic spikes in flakiness (network blips, transient infra GC, tokenization flakiness) caused immediate hard failures in the regression gate, triggering noisy rollbacks. This change reduces false-positive blocks while preserving sensitivity to sustained regressions.
Design: - StaggeredProber: new scheduler that accepts a probe set and a window configuration (slot_count, jitter_seed, priority_buckets). It assigns probes to slots dete...

title: Introduce tenant zone-locker middleware, operator KMS lease shim, and audit-stripper profiles
preview: Context: Customers in regulated regions require per-tenant enforcement that incoming requests are pinned to an allowed geo zone and that any KMS operations use an ephemeral operator lease tied to the asserted zone. This PR implements a lightweight "zone-locker" middleware to normalize/respect tenant zone assertions,...
text: Context: Customers in regulated regions require per-tenant enforcement that incoming requests are pinned to an allowed geo zone and that any KMS operations use an ephemeral operator lease tied to the asserted zone. This PR implements a lightweight "zone-locker" middleware to normalize/respect tenant zone assertions, an operator KMS lease shim to create short-lived operator-scoped credentials for cross-tenant KMS orchestration, and an "audit-stripper" profile that redacts sensitive context from high-volume logs while preserving compliance anchors. Motivation: reduce blast radius for cross-region operator actions, make KMS usage auditable and proofable, and lower PII exposure in high-cardinality audit streams. Changes include: 1) request-time zone assertor that enforces tenant zone from token/headers with configurable soft/hard modes; 2) operator KMS lease flow that leverages existing C...

title: Enforce credential-usage-policy: KMS claim translator & compact audit zipper
preview: Motivation: tighten operator credential usage and KMS selection so that operator/agent tokens cannot be used to access keys outside approved jurisdictions, while keeping audit trails compact and verifiable. This PR adds a lightweight RBAC v2 credential-usage policy evaluator, a KMS claim translator that maps operato...
text:
Motivation: tighten operator credential usage and KMS selection so that operator/agent tokens cannot be used to access keys outside approved jurisdictions, while keeping audit trails compact and verifiable. This PR adds a lightweight RBAC v2 credential-usage policy evaluator, a KMS claim translator that maps operator claims to region-bound key selectors, and a compact audit zipper that collapses repetitive audit entries while retaining a cryptographic verification header for later replay. These features together address customer requests for stricter jurisdictional controls (Linear ENG-2148) and an incident postmortem recommendation to reduce audit storage costs without losing forensic detail (SEC-1203).

What this changes: single continuous description below —
- RBAC: add new policy predicate credential_usage.allowed_actions and integrate into decision path for operator-scoped tokens...

title: Tolerate trailing newline in API key and improve auth error messaging
preview: Context: SUP-4902. Customers reading API keys from mounted files often include a trailing newline. The Go SDK previously validated the API key string strictly and returned a generic 'invalid api key' error, causing confusion. Changes: if key ends with CR/LF, trim only those characters; if other whitespace exists, re...
text: Context: SUP-4902. Customers reading API keys from mounted files often include a trailing newline. The Go SDK previously validated the API key string strictly and returned a generic 'invalid api key' error, causing confusion. Changes: if key ends with CR/LF, trim only those characters; if other whitespace exists, return a clearer error message pointing to the whitespace; add unit tests; update README guidance for Kubernetes secret mounts. Release: v0.6.2.

title: Add language‑agnostic presubmit adaptor and shrinkwrap CI runner
preview: Context: Our presubmit surface has accumulated language-specific runners and duplicated caching logic (JS/TS, Go, Python). This increases maintenance cost and causes inconsistent preflight behavior. This PR introduces a small, language-agnostic presubmit adaptor plus a CI-side shrinkwrap locker to reduce dev depende...
text:
Context: Our presubmit surface has accumulated language-specific runners and duplicated caching logic (JS/TS, Go, Python). This increases maintenance cost and causes inconsistent preflight behavior. This PR introduces a small, language-agnostic presubmit adaptor plus a CI-side shrinkwrap locker to reduce dev dependency churn and shrink the presubmit footprint.

Goals: unify presubmit invocation across languages, centralize cache & auth plumbing, reduce CI setup time per job, and add a deterministic shrinkwrap snapshot for devdeps that CI can use to stabilize installs. Avoid feature creep: adaptor is intentionally minimal and implements a plugin discovery contract for linters/test runners rather than bundling language tools.

What this change does (high level):
- Add tools/presubmit/adaptor: a lightweight Go binary that discovers language plugins, normalizes tool exit codes, and stream...
